title: Retro-orbital Injection of Virus or Dye in Mice
author: Boaz Mohar
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.5udg6s6

steps.title: Prepare either virus dilution (5
steps.instruction: Prepare either virus dilution (5.0e11-1.0e12) with PBS prepare or a dye aliquot . Injection is up to 200ul per mouse.

steps.title: Take one mouse from the cage...
steps.instruction: Take one mouse from the cage into an induction chamber and start isoflurane at 3% with flow rate of ~1L/min

steps.title: After the mouse has slow breathing...
steps.instruction: After the mouse has slow breathing (~1/s) move the mouse to a nose cone delivery of isoflurane at 1.5% with around the same flow rate.

steps.title: Expose the eye ball with two...
steps.instruction: Expose the eye ball with two fingers (above and below) and make sure the you are not pressing on the trachea and the mouse's breathing is regular.

steps.title: Use a 29G syringe at a...
steps.instruction: Use a 29G syringe at a 30-degree angle beveled downwards until you hit the bone, retract a bit and slowly inject. See cited paper for more details. Retro-orbital injections in mice. LabAnimal. 29G x 12.7mm 0.3ml syringe Ulticare Catalog #09230

steps.title: Remove the needle slowly and press...
steps.instruction: Remove the needle slowly and press the eyelid shut for a few seconds.

steps.title: Validating the injection was successful is...
steps.instruction: Validating the injection was successful is possible with JF525 by looking at the pee of the animal after it wakes up. Leaving the animal in a clean cup with kim-wips will collect the pee and it would seem pink.
